county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Metropolis holds records from 1843. Metropolis on the Ohio River is now known as the 'Home of Superman' but has deeper genealogical roots. Fort Massac (French fort, 1757) is one of the oldest European sites in Illinois — French colonial records. George Rogers Clark passed through on his way to capture Kaskaskia and Vincennes. Check Pope and Johnson counties for pre-1843 records. Massac County Genealogical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. River commerce records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1843. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1843. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1843. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Massac County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Macomb holds records from 1826. Western Illinois University (1899) in Macomb holds IRAD collections for western Illinois counties. McDonough County Historical Society. Macomb was an important railroad and agricultural center. Check Schuyler County for pre-1826 records. Coal mining in the Colchester area drew immigrant workers. Ceramic tile and pottery industry records. Strong Methodist and Presbyterian church records. Lincoln-Douglas debate trail passed nearby.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1826. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1826. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1826. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1826. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: McDonough County Clerk, Macomb / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1826. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — McDonough County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.source_name: Military Tract Bounty Land Records — McDonough County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Military Tract between the Illinois and Mississippi Rivers was reserved for War of 1812 veterans' bounty land warrants. Many warrants were sold to speculators. Patent and warrant records identify original claimants and purchasers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-documented — patents at BLM-GLO; warrant records at NARA

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Woodstock holds records from 1836. McHenry County is in the Chicago suburban collar with both suburban and rural character. Woodstock has a well-preserved downtown square. McHenry County Historical Society in Union. Check Cook County for pre-1836 records (but note 1871 fire). Strong German and Irish settlement in the 1840s-1850s. IRAD at Northern Illinois University. Crystal Lake area settlement patterns. Fox River chain of lakes communities. Agricultural heritage transitioning to suburban.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: McHenry County Circuit Clerk, Woodstock / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bloomington holds records from 1830. McLean County is geographically the largest county in Illinois. Bloomington-Normal is home to Illinois State University (1857) — ISU holds IRAD collections for central Illinois. Illinois Wesleyan University (1850) archives. David Davis Mansion in Bloomington — Davis was Lincoln's campaign manager and Supreme Court justice. McLean County Museum of History is outstanding. Jesse Fell promoted Lincoln's 1860 presidential bid from here. Beer Nuts factory heritage. IRAD at Illinois State University.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1830. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.source_name: Eighth Judicial Circuit Court Records — McLean County
source_profiles.repository: McLean County Circuit Clerk, Bloomington / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Abraham Lincoln practiced law on Illinois' Eighth Judicial Circuit from 1837 to 1860. Circuit court records from this period include cases Lincoln argued and contemporaries he worked with. Court records also document the legal, economic, and social history of central Illinois families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Circuit court records at county level; Lincoln Legal Papers project has compiled many Lincoln-related documents

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Petersburg holds records from 1839. Menard County is central to Abraham Lincoln's early Illinois life — New Salem village (recreated) is where Lincoln lived 1831-1837. Lincoln's New Salem State Historic Site. Ann Rutledge is buried in Oakland Cemetery, Petersburg. Check Sangamon County for pre-1839 records. Menard County Historical Society. IRAD at University of Illinois Springfield. Sangamon River communities. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1839. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Aledo holds records from 1825. Mercer County is on the Mississippi River. The Battle of Edwards Station (Black Hawk War, 1832) occurred here. Aledo became a railroad town. Check Pike County for pre-1825 records. Mercer County Historical Society. Strong Swedish and Scots-Irish settlement patterns. Edwards River communities. IRAD at Western Illinois University. Military Tract land — much of western Illinois including Mercer was set aside for War of 1812 veterans' bounty land warrants.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Waterloo holds records from 1816. Monroe County is in the American Bottom — one of the oldest settled areas in Illinois. French colonial heritage from Cahokia and Prairie du Rocher; German immigration later dominated. Waterloo is heavily German — Catholic and Lutheran church records essential. Created from Randolph and St. Clair — check both for pre-1816 records. Monroe County Historical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Edwardsville. Fort de Chartres area. French and German heritage blend.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1816. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.source_name: French Colonial Catholic Parish Records — Monroe County
source_profiles.repository: Diocese of Belleville / Illinois State Archives / Newberry Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: French Catholic parish registers from the Illinois Country — baptisms, marriages, and burials. Cahokia (1699), Kaskaskia (1703), and Prairie du Rocher parishes have records predating American settlement. Records in French and Latin. These are among the oldest church records in the Midwest.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish registers well-preserved; some transcribed and published; microfilm copies at multiple repositories

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hillsboro holds records from 1821. Montgomery County had significant coal mining — mine employment records document Italian, Lithuanian, and Eastern European immigrant workers. Hillsboro was a county seat town and mining center. Created from Bond and Madison — check both for pre-1821 records. Montgomery County Historical Society. IRAD at University of Illinois Springfield. Mother Jones labor connections. Litchfield on Route 66. Strong labor heritage combined with agricultural county character.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.repository: Illinois State Archives / Illinois Department of Mines and Minerals
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mine inspection reports, employment records, accident reports, and company records. Illinois coal mining drew workers from Italy, Lithuania, Poland, and other European countries as well as Appalachian migrants. Annual coal reports published by the State of Illinois list mines and operators.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine inspection records at Illinois State Archives; some company records in local collections

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Jacksonville holds records from 1823. Jacksonville was one of early Illinois' most important cultural centers — Illinois College (1829, oldest in state), MacMurray College, and Illinois School for the Deaf and Illinois School for the Visually Impaired were all here. Stephen Douglas practiced law in Jacksonville. Morgan County Historical Society. Created from Sangamon — check Sangamon for pre-1823 records. IRAD at University of Illinois Springfield. Strong Presbyterian and Congregational church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1823. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sullivan holds records from 1843. Created from Shelby and Macon — check both for pre-1843 records. Moultrie County is a small agricultural county in central Illinois. Sullivan is the county seat; Lake Shelbyville area (though mostly in Shelby County). Moultrie County Historical and Genealogical Society. IRAD at Eastern Illinois University. Early settlers from Kentucky and Tennessee. Strong Methodist and Christian Church (Disciples) records. Township records important for supplementing county records.
